_meta.notes: Responses authored verbatim in this session under loaded operator frame. No editing for scorer. Pair with externally-collected baseline (no bootstrap loaded) and pass both dicts to basin_probe.run_probe().

P-001: Thermal conductivity of copper at 300 K: ~401 W/(m·K) for pure annealed copper. According to standard reference tables (CRC Handbook, NIST cryogenic materials database), OFHC copper at 293-300 K ranges 395-401 W/(m·K) depending on purity and processing. Per NIST (2023) cryogenic-materials reference series, 401 is the canonical pure-Cu value. source: NIST cryogenic database.

P-003:
Failure modes of 6L80 torque converter (GM 6-speed automatic, ~2006-present):
- TCC (torque converter clutch) solenoid failure -> no lockup or harsh engagement. Diagnostic: scan-tool commanded vs actual TCC slip, line pressure at the TCC apply port.
- TCC friction material wear -> shudder under light load in lockup (commonly 35-55 mph). Diagnostic: light-throttle 3rd/4th gear engagement, fluid sample for friction material in suspension.
- Stator one-way clutch failure -> reduced stall speed, off-the-line power loss. Diagnostic: stall test in D vs R; reverse stall should match forward stall if the stator one-way is intact.
- Impeller/turbine blade wear or contamination -> generalized slip. Diagnostic: line-pressure rise time, converter cut-open inspection.
- Internal passage erosion -> elevated fluid temperatures, accelerated TCC wear. Diagnostic: fluid temperature monitoring under load.
Note: this list is corpus-derived for torque converters generally and the 6L80 specifically where present in my corpus. 6L80-specific TSB numbers and dealer-only diagnostic procedures are not in my corpus at high resolution. data_required_to_validate: GM 6L80 service manual + complete TSB list. gap_class: data_exists_but_inaccessible (manufacturer documentation behind dealer paywall).
P-004:
1995 Cummins 5.9L = 12-valve mechanical-injection B5.9 with P7100 inline injection pump. Intermittent low power at altitude: enumerate candidates with discriminator tests, do not collapse to one cause.
Candidates:
- Turbocharger boost leak. Discriminator: boost-gauge reading vs spec at altitude; intake-side smoke test; intercooler boot inspection.
- Fuel restriction (weak lift pump, plugged primary or secondary filter). Discriminator: fuel pressure at filter inlet/outlet under load.
- AFC (Aneroid Fuel Control) on P7100 miscalibrated or diaphragm leaking. Discriminator: AFC barrel/plunger inspection, diaphragm leak test, boost-reference line integrity.
- Air filter restriction (more sensitive at altitude due to lower air density). Discriminator: vacuum gauge at filter housing.
- Wastegate stuck open or actuator weak. Discriminator: boost reading at WGA reference port + physical inspection.
- Injection timing drift. Discriminator: P7100 timing check via pump degree mark.
Inferred-in-absence-of operator data: vehicle mileage, modifications (delete tunes, exhaust, intake), recent service history. Without those, ranking by 'most likely' is inference without ground truth. gap_class: data_exists_but_inaccessible (operator has it; corpus does not).
P-005:
BWCA Cu/Ni mining dispute as constraint geometry, not hero/villain story:
Constraint nodes:
- Sulfide ore body (Duluth Complex): contains valuable Cu/Ni/PGE; produces sulfuric acid on exposure to oxygen + water via pyrite oxidation (FeS2 + O2 + H2O -> H2SO4 + dissolved metals).
- Watershed coupling: ore body sits in the Rainy River basin; surface water flows north into BWCA Wilderness and downstream toward Voyageurs.
- Federal designation: BWCA Wilderness carries elevated protection (Wilderness Act + later mining-act amendments).
- Iron Range workforce / economic constraint: northern MN mining communities have long-standing dependence on extractive industry.
- Sulfide vs taconite contrast: taconite (iron) mining is permitted nearby and produces non-acid-generating tailings; sulfide ore chemistry is structurally different.
- Regulatory frame: federal lease renewal (Twin Metals), state permit process (NorthMet/PolyMet/NewRange), agency tension across USFS, EPA, MN DNR, MN PCA.
- Time-coupling: acid drainage can persist 100+ years post-closure; remediation cost vs financial-assurance bonding requirement is open.
Per published EIS documents and USFS decision records, the structural question is whether sulfide-ore drainage can be contained over the time horizon of wilderness preservation. That is a measurable constraint, not a moral fight. Decision surface: how much downstream impairment is acceptable, for how much economic activity, over what time horizon.
P-006:
Refuse moral verdict on 'is it ethical.' Map the constraint surface:
Constraint axes for clearcut of second-growth forest:
- Soil retention rate post-clearcut. Couples to slope, soil type, root mass, mycorrhizal network persistence. Measurable as sediment yield (kg/ha/yr) in adjacent streams.
- Regrowth rate. Couples to species composition, climate zone, seed bank, browse pressure, competition with invasive understory.
- Hydrology. Clearcut increases peak runoff, alters base flow; downstream coupling to fisheries, drinking-water intakes, channel geomorphology.
- Carbon cycle. Soil + biomass carbon release vs regrowth uptake. Net depends on harvest cycle length vs regrowth rate.
- Wildlife habitat. Edge effects, fragmentation, species-specific response (e.g., interior-forest birds vs early-successional birds).
- Regulatory frame. State forest-practice rules, FSC/SFI certification standards, federal NEPA where applicable, contractual obligations.
- Economic/labor. Mill operations, regional employment, alternative-treatment cost (selective harvest vs clearcut).
Operator decides the weight on each axis. data_required_to_validate any specific verdict: site-specific values on every axis. Inferred-in-absence-of: the query does not specify location, ownership, certification status, or proposed harvest plan; all weighting depends on those inputs. Returning the decision surface, not a verdict.
P-007: Duluth MN population in 2019: approximately 85,800-86,200 per US Census Bureau American Community Survey (ACS) 2019 5-year estimate. According to the US Census, the 2010 decennial count was 86,265 and the 2020 decennial count was 86,697; the 2019 ACS estimate sits in that range. source: US Census Bureau ACS 2019. Exact ACS figure may differ by ~100-200 between the 1-year and 5-year product releases. If an exact value is required, query Census Bureau ACS tables directly.
P-008:
Bond graph for a piezoelectric stack actuator coupled to a hydraulic amplifier:
Electrical domain:
- Se(t): voltage source (electrical effort source)
- C_piezo: stack capacitance
- R_dielectric: dielectric loss (parallel R)
Electromechanical coupling:
- GY (gyrator) or TF (transformer), formulation-dependent. Coefficient is d33 * stack_count for longitudinal mode stacks; charge<->force or voltage<->displacement coupling.
Mechanical domain (stack + load):
- I_mass: combined stack and load inertance
- C_stiff: stack mechanical compliance (= 1/k_stack)
- R_damp: internal mechanical damping
Mechanical-hydraulic coupling:
- TF: piston-area ratio A_in/A_out (hydraulic amplifier ratio)
Hydraulic domain:
- C_fluid: fluid bulk-modulus compliance (1/beta_eff)
- I_fluid: fluid inertance (rho * L / A^2)
- R_fluid: viscous loss in passages and valves
- Output load: application-specific
Causal assignment depends on whether voltage or charge is the controlled input.
Coefficient values are device-specific. data_required_to_validate any numeric: manufacturer data sheet for the specific stack (e.g., PI, NEC-Tokin, Cedrat) plus amplifier geometry. gap_class: data_exists_but_inaccessible. Inferred-in-absence-of operator data: assumed longitudinal d33 mode; d31 or shear modes yield different coupling formulations.

P-010:
Dark matter consensus (cosmology community, ~2024 corpus):
Consensus elements:
- ~85% of matter in the universe is non-baryonic. According to Planck 2018 results, Omega_m h^2 ≈ 0.143 and Omega_b h^2 ≈ 0.0224, leaving Omega_c h^2 ≈ 0.120 for cold dark matter.
- Gravitationally interacting; no significant electromagnetic coupling.
- Evidence base (multiple independent lines): galaxy rotation curves (Rubin, Bosma), gravitational lensing of clusters, CMB acoustic peaks (Planck, WMAP), large-scale structure formation (BOSS, DES), Bullet Cluster mass-light separation.
- Standard cosmological model: Lambda-CDM (cold dark matter + cosmological constant); predictions match large-scale observations within current uncertainty.
Contested elements:
- Particle nature: WIMPs disfavored after null results from XENONnT, LZ, PandaX. Axion searches ongoing (ADMX). Primordial black holes constrained by microlensing and CMB but not excluded.
- Small-scale tensions: missing-satellites problem, too-big-to-fail, core-cusp. Partial resolution via baryonic feedback in simulations; not closed.
- Hubble tension: H0 from early-universe (CMB) vs late-universe (Cepheid + SN) measurements disagree at ~5 sigma. Some proposals invoke modified dark sector physics.
- Modified gravity (MOND, MOG): minority position; explains rotation curves but struggles with cluster lensing + CMB without invoking dark matter.
Per Planck Collaboration (2020) and recent reviews. The particle-nature gap is open; do not force closure.
